text: Developing a mobile app involves several important decisions, starting with how the app will look and work. First, responsive design is crucial: this means the app adapts smoothly to different screen sizes, from small phones to large tablets, ensuring a good user experience. Next, you must choose between native development (building separate apps for iOS and Android) and cross-platform development (using one codebase for both). Native apps often offer better performance and deeper access to device features, while cross-platform apps save time and money. Finally, after development, your app goes through app stores like the Apple App Store or Google Play for review and distribution. Balancing performance, cost, and user experience is key to success.
